name: Filets of Sole Riviera
recipeIngredient: 3 Large Green peppers, roasted, seeded and cut into strips
3 Large Red peppers, roasted, seeded and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Do not peel the tomatoes; cut them into thick slices and sprinkle with salt. Let them drain on a large plate for 30 minutes.
2. Dip the sole filets in milk. Dry them lightly with paper towels.
3. Season the flour with salt and white pepper. Dip the filets in the seasoned flour, then transfer to beaten eggs, covering them well. Coat thoroughly with bread crumbs.
4. Place the filets on a large platter and refrigerate for 1 hour.
5. Twenty minutes before serving, heat 3 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet. Add roasted pepper strips and cook over high heat for 2-3 minutes, seasoning with salt and black pepper. Transfer to a serving platter and keep warm.
6. In another skillet, melt 4 tablespoons butter with 2 tablespoons oil. Add the filets two at a time and sauté over medium heat for 3 minutes.
7. Turn the filets carefully without breaking through the coating and continue sautéing for 3-4 more minutes.
8. Regulate the heat to prevent burning. Add a little more olive oil to the pan used for peppers, then add tomato slices and cook quickly until heated through (about 2 minutes).
9. Remove the pan from the heat. Place the cooked filets on the bed of peppers. Top with tomato slices, garnish with anchovy fillets, quartered lemons, and parsley.
10. In a small saucepan, melt softened butter over low heat. Add anchovy paste and lemon juice; pour over the sole and tomatoes and serve immediately.
